notes: Spec asserts: state stays working through the queued-message arrival, no →ready flicker when the queued input lands, the cycle still ends at ready. The recipe asks gemini to count slowly (so the working span is long enough to type into); the driver then types a second message via the keys step (tmux send-keys + Enter) mid-turn. Gemini CLI's useMessageQueue hook buffers that text in React state (messageQueue) and flushes it to submitQuery ONLY when streamingState reaches idle — so the queued text is NOT written to the per-session JSONL transcript until the current turn completes. The gemini-cli parser is transcript-shape-driven (turn_done only on a final text-only 'gemini' message with no tool calls), so it sees one contiguous working span and emits no premature settle. The queued_window phase pins the no-flicker invariant with duration_at_least_ms 3000. The recording does NOT assert the consumption shape — Gemini joins all queued lines and submits them as a single follow-up turn at idle; the spec accommodates that. observations.model ('gemini-3-flash-preview') is the live-verified default boot model and is PROVISIONAL — record settles it. DRIVER NOTE: needs the keys primitive (driver=gap:keys); record ports it from drive-claudecode-interactive.sh before driving.

text: Session appears in ready shortly after launch
First user prompt drives ready → working

A second message is typed into the TUI mid-turn (keys step) and queued in Gemini's useMessageQueue React state. State must stay working — no flicker to ready when the queued input lands (the queued text is not written to the transcript until streaming idle), no flicker to waiting (spec bullets 1 and 2)
The cycle eventually ends at ready (spec bullet 3) — at streaming idle the queued message is flushed as a single follow-up turn (ready → working → ready); the final state is ready
